top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Alberto Contador's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
92ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Gran Fondo Alberto Contador's paved Mediterranean mountain roads through the forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 91 miles.
8,327ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At mile 60 of 91, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
At Gran Fondo Alberto Contador, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Alberto Contador. Train it until it feels boring.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Gran Fondo Alberto Contador, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
8,327ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved Mediterranean mountain roads through the. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 8,327ft of climbing on paved Mediterranean mountain roads through the does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 91 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Alberto Contador’s 91 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Gran Fondo Alberto Contador. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Gran Fondo Alberto Contador requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on paved mediterranean mountain roads through the costa blanca interior to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, TT Threshold, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 91 miles of paved mediterranean mountain roads through the costa blanca interior in Oliva, Valencia, Spain.
